title: AIAM-1 Identity Claim
description: Composite identity claim for an AI agent, comprising model provenance, orchestration layer, goal context, and principal. Identity claims are independently signed (ID-050) — unlike intent claims, which are signed only as part of attestation records.

spec_version.description: AIAM-1 specification version this identity claim was authored against.

claim_id.description: Unique identifier for this identity claim.

expires_at.description: Timestamp of claim expiration. Claims without explicit expiration MUST NOT be accepted (ID-051).

signature.description: Cryptographic signature of the issuing authority over the claim contents.

model_version.description: Specific model version or checkpoint identifier.

model_attestation.description: Cryptographic hash of model artifact (sha256:...) or signed provider attestation. MUST be verifiable without access to model weights (ID-011).

provider.description: Organization that produced or hosts the model.

fine_tuning_id.description: Identifier for fine-tuning applied on top of base model.

framework_version.description: Specific version of the agent framework.

harness_hash.description: Cryptographic hash of the agent harness configuration (system prompts, tool definitions, memory configuration). MUST — any change to harness_hash triggers identity claim reissuance (ID-021).

deployment_id.description: Unique identifier for this deployment instance. MUST for replicated deployments (ID-022); SHOULD for single-instance.

goal_id.description: Unique identifier for this goal context. Referenced by intent claims via goal_ref.

purpose.description: Structured statement of the purpose for which the agent was instantiated. Minimum 20 characters.

scope.description: Operational boundaries: what the agent is authorized to work on. Minimum 20 characters — scopes under 20 characters or lacking resource/action qualifiers are candidates for rejection during conformance assessment (ID-031).

constraints.description: Explicit restrictions on agent behavior within this goal context.

expiry.description: Time at which this goal context expires.

contact.description: Contact information for the accountable party.

jurisdiction.description: Legal jurisdiction under which the principal operates (e.g., 'US-VA', 'EU', 'GB').

extensions.description: Top-level vendor-specific or implementation-specific identity metadata.
